text: Social media platforms should ban political misinformation because false political claims can mislead voters, damage democratic decision-making, and spread rapidly before corrections reach the same audience.
Banning political misinformation is justified when content makes demonstrably false claims about elections, voting procedures, public safety, or democratic institutions.
Platforms already moderate spam, fraud, and harmful manipulation, so removing political misinformation is a reasonable extension of existing content governance.
Social media platforms should not ban political misinformation because private companies may overreach, suppress controversial opinions, and become arbiters of political truth.
Banning political misinformation risks censorship because political claims are often disputed, context-dependent, or later corrected by new evidence.
Instead of banning political misinformation, platforms should prioritize counterspeech, fact-check labels, transparency, and user education while preserving open political debate.
